titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 606

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=929 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:47:55.674358+00 | translation moa=928 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:47:55.682306+00

div.p:
. W . dei gracia Rex Scott’ . Omnibus Probis Hominibus tocius terre sue Clericis et Laicis Salutem . Sciant presentes et futuri me dedisse et concessisse et hac carta mea confirmasse deo et ecclesie de Coldingham et
monachis ibidem deo seruientibus unum plenariam toftum in burgo meo de Hadingt’ . Tenendum
in liberam puram et perpetuam Elemosinam ita libere et Quiete plenarie et honorifice sicut alie
domus relligiose tofta sua in aliis burgis meis liberius quietius plenius et honorificentius te
nent et possident . Testibus . H . Cancellario meo . Ricardo de prebenda clerico meo . Walter capellano meo .
Henrico de graham . Adam hareng’ . Bernardo de Hauden’ . Roberto le Bastard’. Apud Seleschirc’.

p.cl: William by the grace of God king of the Scots to all worthy men of his entire land greeting.

Let present and future know me to have given and granted and by this my charter to have made firm to God and to the church of Coldingham and the monks serving God there one full toft in my burgh of Haddington, to be held in free, pure and perpetual alms as freely and quitly, fully and honourably, as other religious houses freely, quitly, fully and honourably hold and possess their own tofts in my other burghs.
As witnesses, Hugh my chancellor, Richard de Prebenda my clerk, Walter my chaplain, Henry of Graham, Adam of Hastings, Bernard of Howden, Robert the Bastard.
